name: 7-Simul
description: The 7-Simul method is an expert-level Clock solving technique used for world-record-level solves. It solves the entire Clock in just seven moves, each of which simultaneously affects clocks on both the front and back faces. Crucially, it requires no flip, meaning the solver never turns the puzzle over. This method demands exceptional mental calculation and deep understanding of Clock mechanics.

steps.name: Analyze Scramble
steps.description: During inspection, calculate all seven moves needed to solve the Clock. Each move must be planned to simultaneously correct clocks on both faces, and the entire solution must be computed before execution begins.
tips: This step is entirely mental — no physical moves are made
Develop a systematic approach to calculating simultaneous front/back corrections
Practice mental math for clock arithmetic (mod 12)
Speed of analysis directly determines solve time

steps.name: Execute 7 Simultaneous Moves
steps.description: Execute exactly seven pre-planned moves. Each move involves setting pins and turning wheels to simultaneously solve clocks on both the front and back faces. No flip is performed at any point during the solve.
tips: Execute moves as quickly as possible — all planning is done
Develop fast and precise finger techniques for pin pressing and wheel turning
Practice the physical execution separately from the mental planning

pros: Fastest possible Clock method — used for world records
No flip required, saving significant time
Only 7 moves for a complete solve
cons: Requires complete mastery of Clock mechanics and interactions
Very steep learning curve even for experienced Clock solvers
Inspection time limits can be a constraint in competition
